response.justification: The candidate is a strong, versatile senior engineer with genuine systems-programming depth, but his profile does not match this role's core requirement: hands-on modern C++ expertise. His only professional C++ experience was a ~7-month stint at National Instruments ending in 2015, C++ is absent from his current skills list, and his recent decade centers on TypeScript/Node.js web platforms. He also lacks the industrial-automation domain: no MQTT, no IEC 62443/SDL exposure, no OT protocols, and his cloud experience is AWS rather than Azure. The low-latency DLang work shows transferable concurrency and networking aptitude, but for a role demanding immediate C++/STL architecture leadership and industrial-cybersecurity mentoring, the gap is too wide.
strengths: Real low-latency systems experience (async I/O, fibers, caching) handling millions of requests/second at Dunnhumby/Sociomantic
Long-running ownership of an open-source, event-driven geo-data platform, showing architectural and product maturity
Strong engineering-culture credentials: TDD, CI/CD, mentoring, workshops, and community teaching
concerns: Professional C++ experience is minimal (~7 months) and roughly a decade old; C++ is not listed among current skills
No industrial automation, IoT/MQTT, or IEC 62443 / Secure Development Lifecycle background required by the role
Recent experience is web/full-stack (TypeScript, Node.js, AWS) rather than networked multi-threaded C++ systems; unexplained ~8-month gap in 2020

key_factors.factor: No industrial cybersecurity (IEC 62443/SDL) or MQTT/OT-protocol experience
